Peyronie's disease 
Peyronie's disease is a condition that causes your penis to curve when it's erect. It can be painful but there are treatments to ease the symptoms and stop it getting worse.
Check if it's Peyronie's disease 
Peyronie's disease mainly affects men over 40, but younger men can also get it. It can affect any adult with a penis.
Credit: Multimedia Team - nhs.uk
Symptoms of Peyronie's disease include:
swelling that later causes a hard lump to develop on the shaft of your penis and stops it stretching during erections a curve in your penis when it's erect (if the hard area is on the top of your penis it will curve upwards, if it's on the bottom it will curve downwards) painful erections that can make having sex difficult problems getting or keeping an erection ( erectile dysfunction ) other changes to your penis – for example, you may have a hard area on the top, bottom and side of your penis, shortening it and making it narrower in the middle If you have Peyronie's disease, your skin may also be thicker and less flexible in other parts of your body, such as in your hands and feet.
